sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is utilized across all stages of ampullary adenocarcinoma, including neoadjuvant, adjuvant, and metastatic settings. Treatment selection depends on histologic subtype (pancreatobiliary/mixed vs. intestinal), performance status, and molecular profiling. Most recommendations are extrapolated from pancreatic, biliary tract, and colorectal cancer guidelines due to limited ampullary-specific data [AMP-E, MS-2, MS-6].

cards.name: Albumin-bound paclitaxel/gemcitabine
cards.detail: Setting: Neoadjuvant, adjuvant, first-line metastatic for pancreatobiliary/mixed type. Cycle: 28-day cycle. Toxicities: Neutropenia, fatigue, neuropathy, anemia.

cards.name: Capecitabine/gemcitabine
cards.detail: Setting: Neoadjuvant, adjuvant, first-line metastatic for pancreatobiliary/mixed type. Cycle: (cycle days not fully provided in input; typical 21-day cycle). Toxicities: Hand-foot syndrome, diarrhea, myelosuppression.

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: For good/intermediate PS: pancreatobiliary/mixed type: albumin-bound paclitaxel/gemcitabine, capecitabine/gemcitabine, cisplatin/gemcitabine, cisplatin/gemcitabine + durvalumab, FOLFIRINOX/modified FOLFIRINOX, FOLFOX, or NALIRIFOX. Intestinal type: CAPEOX, CAPEOX + bevacizumab, FOLFIRI, FOLFIRI + bevacizumab, FOLFIRINOX, FOLFIRINOX + bevacizumab, FOLFOX, or FOLFOX + bevacizumab. For poor PS: palliative care preferred; if treated, pancreatobiliary/mixed: capecitabine, fluorouracil/leucovorin, or gemcitabine; intestinal: capecitabine or fluorouracil/leucovorin.
